summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #712 where optional enum MCP tool parameters cause LLMs to send the literal string 'null' instead of a null value, by writing a regression test and patching the strict mode schema handler.
summary.outcome: Root cause identified (two-layer bug: canonicalization strips type from enums, make_nullable had no fallback for enum-only schemas), fix implemented with an add_null_to_enum() helper, 4 regression tests added, e2e scenario added, PR #724 created and a follow-up test added in response to a review comment.

repo: Schema canonicalization in sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat strips 'type': 'string' from enum properties, leaving bare {'enum': [...]} objects — valid JSON Schema but surprising for downstream processors.
strict_mode.rs make_nullable() only handled schemas with a 'type' field or anyOf/oneOf; enum-only schemas (post-canonicalization) fell through silently.
The project uses a 'beads' issue tracker alongside GitHub issues — issues must be claimed and closed there separately.
The workspace has a pre-existing clippy error in moltis-agents unrelated to providers work.
CUDA build targets fail on macOS — expected and unrelated to provider crate changes.
just test / just lint use --features local-llm-metal on macOS.
bd dolt pull errors are expected in worktrees with no Dolt remote configured.

code.finding: make_nullable() needed two fixes: (1) when type field is present and an enum array exists, null must be added to the enum to keep it consistent with the nullable type; (2) a new fallback branch for enum-only schemas (no type/anyOf/oneOf) that adds null directly to the enum array.
sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat removes 'type' from enum schemas during canonicalization, which is the upstream cause of the enum-only fallback path being needed.
New e2e scenario 'issue-712-nullable-enum-params' added to cover Tavily-style MCP schemas with nullable enum parameters in provider integration tests.

workflow: Write a failing test before the fix to confirm the bug is reproduced — the test failing with the expected wrong value validates the reproduction.
Adding a quick debug test inline to inspect intermediate schema state (after canonicalization) is an effective way to understand multi-stage transformations, then remove it before committing.
When clippy or lint fails, check git blame / main branch to confirm whether errors are pre-existing before investigating further.
PR review comments can surface missing test coverage for specific code paths (e.g., the post-canonicalization enum-only path) even when existing tests pass.

friction: Two-layer bug made diagnosis non-obvious: the initial fix (handling type+enum mismatch) was correct but insufficient because canonicalization had already stripped the type field, requiring a second code path.
The e2e test through to_openai_tools initially failed due to enum value reordering from canonicalization, requiring debugging to understand the full pipeline.
bd dolt pull error on commit is noise in every commit workflow for this worktree.
CUDA build errors in workspace-wide test runs on macOS create noise when checking for regressions.
open_items: Pre-existing clippy error in moltis-agents crate is unresolved and unrelated to this fix — should be addressed separately.

The canonicalization behavior of stripping 'type' from enum schemas is a footgun for other downstream processors beyond make_nullable — worth auditing for other places that assume a 'type' field will be present.
